title: Arcton Veyr
text: At the age of 35, Arcton Veyr was a tall man who had blue eyes, from whom Elyra Noctis inherited her one blue eye. He wore an elaborate robe in shades of red made of broad overlapping petals with wide sleeves that covered his hands. Over the robe, he wore a wide gray sash with a pattern of connecting circles. The sign on his sash was the letter "A" in The Luminous Order alphabet, which symbolizes The Luminous Order.
page_content:
Title: Arcton Veyr
Type: character
Section: Overview

At the age of 35, Arcton Veyr was a tall man who had blue eyes, from whom Elyra Noctis inherited her one blue eye. He wore an elaborate robe in shades of red made of broad overlapping petals with wide sleeves that covered his hands. Over the robe, he wore a wide gray sash with a pattern of connecting circles. The sign on his sash was the letter "A" in The Luminous Order alphabet, which symbolizes The Luminous Order.

title: Arcton Veyr
text:
Arcton Veyr wore an elaborate dark blue headdress that resembled an Arabian keffiyeh or desert headgear. It was held in place by a band that had a blue stone in front and a long trail that curved behind him. On each side hung a pale blue tassel. He wore a white beaded bracelet and wielded an invoker's staff.

Arcton Veyr was a kind, cheerful man, desiring peace and unity and willing to sacrifice his life for the future of the world. He loved his wife, never caring that she was The Relicborn, even though their relationship was controversial: she was disowned by her brother and was shunned by the other The Relicborn in addition to the rest of Elyndra and their daughter was disregarded by Luminous faithful and The Relicborn alike. Arcton Veyr cared for his oathsworn, Garron Vale and Darius Veyr, who were his dear friends.
page_content:

Arcton Veyr was a kind, cheerful man, desiring peace and unity and willing to sacrifice his life for the future of the world. He loved his wife, never caring that she was The Relicborn, even though their relationship was controversial: she was disowned by her brother and was shunned by the other The Relicborn in addition to the rest of Elyndra and their daughter was disregarded by Luminous faithful and The Relicborn alike. Arcton Veyr cared for his oathsworn, Garron Vale and Darius Veyr, who were his dear friends.

title: Asterreach
text: The camp site is where all invokers and oathsworn rest before they enter the dome and complete their pilgrimage.
page_content:
Title: Asterreach
Type: location
Section: Overview

The camp site is where all invokers and oathsworn rest before they enter the dome and complete their pilgrimage.

title: Asterreach
text:
The Road to Asterreach is lined with the ruins of fallen buildings and roads. The entire area is covered in ember motes.

The Asterreach Dome is the ruins of the former temple and government seat of Asterreach. Its architecture resembles the skyball stadium in Dream Asterreach.
page_content:

The Asterreach Dome is the ruins of the former temple and government seat of Asterreach. Its architecture resembles the skyball stadium in Dream Asterreach.

title: Aurelion
text: Aurelion is a large, avian creature notable for her dragon-like wings. She primarily attacks with her strong talons. Some of her body is covered in red feathers and she has a long lizard-like tail.
page_content:

Type: aeon
Section: Overview

Aurelion is a large, avian creature notable for her dragon-like wings. She primarily attacks with her strong talons. Some of her body is covered in red feathers and she has a long lizard-like tail.

title: Caelan Veyr
text: He is a rising star skyball player from Asterreach and the son of major skyball star Darius Veyr. After the gargantuan monster known as The Hollow Eclipse attacks his hometown, Caelan Veyr is transported to the world of Elyndra. Lost, confused, and seemingly out of place, Caelan Veyr meets a newly fledged invoker named Elyra Noctis and her oathsworn. Elyra Noctis is to set out on a pilgrimage to put an end to The Hollow Eclipse and, by joining Elyra Noctis on her pilgrimage, Caelan Veyr hopes he will find his way home. Caelan Veyr is upbeat and initially childish and naive to the world of Elyndra. He is also selfless and will do what he believes is right, even if he doesn't think it through first. Throughout the pilgrimage, he matures and becomes more level-headed and grounded.
page_content:
Title: Caelan Veyr
Type: character
Section: Overview

He is a rising star skyball player from Asterreach and the son of major skyball star Darius Veyr. After the gargantuan monster known as The Hollow Eclipse attacks his hometown, Caelan Veyr is transported to the world of Elyndra. Lost, confused, and seemingly out of place, Caelan Veyr meets a newly fledged invoker named Elyra Noctis and her oathsworn. Elyra Noctis is to set out on a pilgrimage to put an end to The Hollow Eclipse and, by joining Elyra Noctis on her pilgrimage, Caelan Veyr hopes he will find his way home. Caelan Veyr is upbeat and initially childish and naive to the world of Elyndra. He is also selfless and will do what he believes is right, even if he doesn't think it through first. Throughout the pilgrimage, he matures and becomes more level-headed and grounded.

title: Caelan Veyr
text: To Caelan Veyr's dismay, he has similarities to his father, who tended to ignore responsibility, take things easy, and never worry about the present situation instead of leaving things for future. Caelan Veyr's resemblance to his father is pointed out by Garron Vale, who says that Caelan Veyr is "the one running away". A lot of Caelan Veyr's personality is shaped by the comments that he used to receive from his father and how he grew up to resent him. Despite having been verbally offensive, at heart, Darius Veyr was proud of his son and his resolve. Caelan Veyr confronts his childhood and his relationship with his father when living out his new life in Elyndra and matures from a self-centered and outspoken athlete to a selfless hero.

Despite having been verbally offensive, at heart, Darius Veyr was proud of his son and his resolve. Caelan Veyr confronts his childhood and his relationship with his father when living out his new life in Elyndra and matures from a self-centered and outspoken athlete to a selfless hero.

Elyndra is preserved in the archive as a location woven into Elyndran history, ritual memory, and the synthetic world model prepared for retrieval.

title: Elyndra
text:
Another form of occupation is in voluntary work, such as the invoker / oathsworn pilgrimages and various religious temple work.

The temple of The Luminous Order dominates Elyndran life, spreading its teachings and acting as a benevolent protector of a much-diminished world—a world where technological and social progress has stalled for 1000 years. Yet, beneath the surface unity of the Elyndran mainland, distrust and old anger still boil, leading one to wonder if the various warring factions wouldn't blow each other to smithereens without The Hollow Eclipse's pacifying presence. Only skyball truly unites the world around a common passion and annual tournaments are held in Lucent Harbor, left untouched by The Hollow Eclipse's wrath mostly due to the Wardens of Dawn's efforts.

The Luminous Order script is related to Archon Velis and the faith of The Luminous Order and is used in temples and inside The Hollow Eclipse. Cactuars have their own language, with Benzo being the only human interpreter.
page_content:

Section: Details

Another form of occupation is in voluntary work, such as the invoker / oathsworn pilgrimages and various religious temple work.

The temple of The Luminous Order dominates Elyndran life, spreading its teachings and acting as a benevolent protector of a much-diminished world—a world where technological and social progress has stalled for 1000 years. Yet, beneath the surface unity of the Elyndran mainland, distrust and old anger still boil, leading one to wonder if the various warring factions wouldn't blow each other to smithereens without The Hollow Eclipse's pacifying presence. Only skyball truly unites the world around a common passion and annual tournaments are held in Lucent Harbor, left untouched by The Hollow Eclipse's wrath mostly due to the Wardens of Dawn's efforts.

The Luminous Order script is related to Archon Velis and the faith of The Luminous Order and is used in temples and inside The Hollow Eclipse. Cactuars have their own language, with Benzo being the only human interpreter.

title: Elyndra
text: The teachings of The Luminous Order, said to have been left by Asterreach's leader, Archon Velis, to his daughter, Lady Yunalesca, were implemented by Veylspire to maintain order through giving the people hope that Elyndra may someday be free of The Hollow Eclipse should they atone. Until Elyra Noctis exposes their lies, the temples teach that relic engines is forbidden, that The Hollow Eclipse is a result of humanity's pride and use of relic engines in the past, and that The Hollow Eclipse can only be vanquished when humanity has attained purity and had been cleansed of past sins. Until that promised time would come, only the ritual known as the Last Invocation would provide a reprieve from The Hollow Eclipse's terror (referred to as "The Truce of Ash ").

title: Elyra Noctis
text: Elyra Noctis is preserved in the archive as a character woven into Elyndran history, ritual memory, and the synthetic world model prepared for retrieval.

Title: Elyra Noctis
Type: character
Section: Overview

Elyra Noctis is preserved in the archive as a character woven into Elyndran history, ritual memory, and the synthetic world model prepared for retrieval.

title: Elyra Noctis
text: Elyra Noctis has shoulder-length brunette hair. She wears a blue-beaded earring on her right ear, hidden by a lock of her hair, woven into blue beads with gold rings around the larger, middle bead. She wears a silver necklace of a hibiscus flower, along with a matching ring on the pinky of her right hand. She also wears a silver ring on the middle finger of her left hand and two bracelets, one beaded and one silver, on her right wrist. Elyra Noctis has heterochromia: her left eye is blue, while the right is green to represent her half-The Relicborn heritage, though without the characteristic spiral.
page_content:
Title: Elyra Noctis
Type: character
Section: Details

Elyra Noctis has shoulder-length brunette hair. She wears a blue-beaded earring on her right ear, hidden by a lock of her hair, woven into blue beads with gold rings around the larger, middle bead. She wears a silver necklace of a hibiscus flower, along with a matching ring on the pinky of her right hand. She also wears a silver ring on the middle finger of her left hand and two bracelets, one beaded and one silver, on her right wrist. Elyra Noctis has heterochromia: her left eye is blue, while the right is green to represent her half-The Relicborn heritage, though without the characteristic spiral.

title: Elyra Noctis
text: Elyra Noctis is a kind, loyal, honest, polite, and passionate humanitarian who strives to see the best in others and has a strong conviction to complete whatever task she feels is her duty. She is naive, always believing in the best of people, and places the needs of others above her own, becoming apologetic when she feels like she has let someone down. Like most invokers, Elyra Noctis is a devout follower of The Luminous Order's teachings. She hides her feelings of fear and sadness while encouraging her friends to express themselves in her place. She is willing to carry others' burdens and sacrifice everything she has to destroy The Hollow Eclipse, giving up chances for her own happiness if it means helping others. Her interactions with Caelan Veyr make her question The Luminous Order's teachings and she becomes more direct and open. When her faith in The Luminous Order is shaken, Elyra Noctis must find her own path in life and pursue her goals without the backing of faith.
page_content:
Title: Elyra Noctis
Type: character
Section: Details

Elyra Noctis is a kind, loyal, honest, polite, and passionate humanitarian who strives to see the best in others and has a strong conviction to complete whatever task she feels is her duty. She is naive, always believing in the best of people, and places the needs of others above her own, becoming apologetic when she feels like she has let someone down. Like most invokers, Elyra Noctis is a devout follower of The Luminous Order's teachings. She hides her feelings of fear and sadness while encouraging her friends to express themselves in her place. She is willing to carry others' burdens and sacrifice everything she has to destroy The Hollow Eclipse, giving up chances for her own happiness if it means helping others. Her interactions with Caelan Veyr make her question The Luminous Order's teachings and she becomes more direct and open. When her faith in The Luminous Order is shaken, Elyra Noctis must find her own path in life and pursue her goals without the backing of faith.

title: Elyra Noctis
text: Elyra Noctis is a talented invoker despite her youth, besting her more experienced peers in eidolon duels (albeit, at times, with her oathsworn's aid). She can even summon during a free fall. She performs the sending ritual to send the souls of the deceased to the Veilward, which involves her dancing and, at one point, walking on the water's surface. She is adept at white magic, providing healing and support.
page_content:
Title: Elyra Noctis

She is adept at white magic, providing healing and support.

title: Garron Vale
text: Garron Vale watches over Caelan Veyr while concealing his mysterious past tying him into the stories of Arcton Veyr and Caelan Veyr's father, Darius Veyr. After Garron Vale takes Caelan Veyr to Elyndra, the latter learns that Garron Vale is revered as one of the greatest oathsworn who ever lived due to serving Arcton Veyr on his successful pilgrimage. He becomes an oathsworn for Elyra Noctis, Arcton Veyr's daughter, on her pilgrimage and is, in many ways, the party's mentor, as the others come to him for advice or ask for his permission.
page_content:
Title: Garron Vale
Type: character
Section: Overview

Garron Vale watches over Caelan Veyr while concealing his mysterious past tying him into the stories of Arcton Veyr and Caelan Veyr's father, Darius Veyr. After Garron Vale takes Caelan Veyr to Elyndra, the latter learns that Garron Vale is revered as one of the greatest oathsworn who ever lived due to serving Arcton Veyr on his successful pilgrimage. He becomes an oathsworn for Elyra Noctis, Arcton Veyr's daughter, on her pilgrimage and is, in many ways, the party's mentor, as the others come to him for advice or ask for his permission.

title: Garron Vale
text:
Garron Vale wears a brown shoulder pad on his left shoulder intricately decorated with tan, green, and blue patterns with a beaded ornament dangling from it. He wears black pants and shoes that have brown straps and triangular metal plating. Garron Vale wears a black shirt with a high gray collar with intersecting brown straps. His hair is black with gray streaks held back by a gold ribbon and he has some stubble on his chin.

Garron Vale is a strict, no-nonsense warrior with a dry wit who carries himself with grace and dignity, maintaining many subtle mannerisms of a traditional samurai. He is the strong silent type, speaking when he feels it's necessary—usually offering his knowledge and insight in relevant situations or sharing stories of his past experiences as a young oathsworn. As a result of his devotion to High Invoker Arcton Veyr, Garron Vale is idolized across Elyndra by many aspiring oathsworn.
page_content:
Title: Garron Vale

Garron Vale is a strict, no-nonsense warrior with a dry wit who carries himself with grace and dignity, maintaining many subtle mannerisms of a traditional samurai. He is the strong silent type, speaking when he feels it's necessary—usually offering his knowledge and insight in relevant situations or sharing stories of his past experiences as a young oathsworn. As a result of his devotion to High Invoker Arcton Veyr, Garron Vale is idolized across Elyndra by many aspiring oathsworn.

title: Garron Vale
text: Garron Vale proves his imperishable loyalty to Arcton Veyr and Darius Veyr, but beneath the silence, he is outraged over the events of ten years ago. He has become cynical, having little-to-no respect for The Luminous Order. Despite his usually collected demeanor, Garron Vale harbors feelings of self-loathing. He claims to Caelan Veyr and Torren Kaid that though he is seen as a great oathsworn, he considers his past self (like them) foolish and young.
page_content:
Title: Garron Vale
Type: character
Section: Details

Garron Vale proves his imperishable loyalty to Arcton Veyr and Darius Veyr, but beneath the silence, he is outraged over the events of ten years ago. He has become cynical, having little-to-no respect for The Luminous Order. Despite his usually collected demeanor, Garron Vale harbors feelings of self-loathing. He claims to Caelan Veyr and Torren Kaid that though he is seen as a great oathsworn, he considers his past self (like them) foolish and young.

title: Garron Vale
text: Garron Vale exhibits some fatherly tendencies with Caelan Veyr and Elyra Noctis due to the promise he made to Darius Veyr and Arcton Veyr that he would look after their children. Garron Vale is patient with Caelan Veyr's childish behavior and insists that he be accepted as an oathsworn to Elyra Noctis, much to others' surprise. Caelan Veyr reminds him of Darius Veyr and Garron Vale enjoys pointing out the similarities, much to Caelan Veyr's annoyance. Garron Vale becomes irritated at hold-ups during the pilgrimage, although he ultimately lets the party do as they please, provided that it doesn't infringe on the journey. He does not hesitate to enter shops operated by the Relicborn or use relic engines, despite The Luminous Order's opposition of them.

title: Isola Veyn
text: A large beach that contains a port. It is the only way on and off Isola Veyn.
page_content:
Title: Isola Veyn
Type: location
Section: Overview

A large beach that contains a port. It is the only way on and off Isola Veyn.

title: Isola Veyn
text:
A large lake in the center of Isola Veyn. It contains a long underwater passage that can be used to reach the village.

Veyn Temple is located in Veyn Village. It is where the eidolon, Aurelion, is obtained from the temple's dreambound, a young girl. It is where Elyra Noctis completes her studies and becomes an invoker.
page_content:
Title: Isola Veyn
Type: location
Section: Details

A large lake in the center of Isola Veyn. It contains a long underwater passage that can be used to reach the village.

Veyn Temple is located in Veyn Village. It is where the eidolon, Aurelion, is obtained from the temple's dreambound, a young girl. It is where Elyra Noctis completes her studies and becomes an invoker.

title: Kaelor Rhun
text: Kaelor, while taller than humans, is smaller than the average Rhunari and has a broken horn. He is a fierce protector of Elyra Noctis and very loyal to her. He is mistrusting of others at first and rarely speaks. He dislikes others trying to solve his problems for him.
page_content:
Title: Kaelor Rhun
Type: character
Section: Overview

Kaelor, while taller than humans, is smaller than the average Rhunari and has a broken horn. He is a fierce protector of Elyra Noctis and very loyal to her. He is mistrusting of others at first and rarely speaks. He dislikes others trying to solve his problems for him.

title: Kaelor Rhun
text: Kaelor has blue fur and white hair and a beard. He is leonine, tall, muscular, and has a tail. He has a broken horn on his forehead, causing him great shame as a Rhunari. His ears are pierced and his hair is held back in a ponytail and braided on the sides. Due to his fur, Kaelor doesn't need to wear clothes for warmth. He wears leather straps that hold in place his arm guards and his chest piece that has a skull motif. He wears ankle guards and a sash tied around his waist with a red belt. His shoulder guards have tufts of white feathers or fur.
page_content:

Kaelor has blue fur and white hair and a beard. He is leonine, tall, muscular, and has a tail. He has a broken horn on his forehead, causing him great shame as a Rhunari. His ears are pierced and his hair is held back in a ponytail and braided on the sides. Due to his fur, Kaelor doesn't need to wear clothes for warmth. He wears leather straps that hold in place his arm guards and his chest piece that has a skull motif. He wears ankle guards and a sash tied around his waist with a red belt. His shoulder guards have tufts of white feathers or fur.

title: Last Invocation
text: The Crown Eidolon ( 究極召喚獣, Kyūkyoku Shōkanjū?, lit. Final Summoned Monster ) is the eidolon an invoker gains at the end of their pilgrimage to Asterreach. The eidolon is intended to be used only once: during the final battle with The Hollow Eclipse, in a ritual called the Last Invocation ( 究極召喚, Kyūkyoku Shōkan?, lit. Last Invocation ).

title: Maelor Voss
text: Maelor is a tall The Sylvan Court - human hybrid who has purple eyes, light blue hair with two long horn-like locks running down his back, and a large bang of hair falling over his face. His ornate robes are unlike the other high preceptors', being predominantly dark blue with red trimmings and a green sash. The robe above Maelor's waist is open, exposing his chest with leonine tattoos, and his sleeves fall to partially cover his hands.

title: Maelor Voss
text:
As a half-The Sylvan Court, Maelor's fingers are more pointed than a human's, but not as long as a The Sylvan Court's. His ears are rounded and human-like, unlike the elfish ears of the Sylvan Court. The veins on Maelor's face are pronounced and there are veins arching over his stomach, another trait passed from the Sylvan Court. Maelor wields a staff in battle, though he is more proficient with magic.

Maelor believes, as many do, that The Hollow Eclipse is eternal and sees death as a release from the sorrow of life. Maelor appears courteous and affable and is popular among Luminous faithful.

The staff that Maelor wields has a unique model. It is simply named Maelor Staff and has the Piercing ability. He also wears a unique piece of armor around his right ankle, simply named Maelor Armor, which has the Sensor ability.

Maelor relies on casting elemental magic and using combo attacks with whichever form of the Mortibody he has with him. He will Banish any eidolon summoned after one turn in the second and third fights and dispel the party all at once in the second, third, and fourth fights.

title: Mount Kaelorn
text: The snow-covered, towering Mount Kaelorn is considered sacred land. Before the Endless Truce only invokers and their oathsworn were permitted to pass beyond the mountain gate as the final test of the invoker's resolve before entering the Asterreach Ruins.
page_content:
Title: Mount Kaelorn
Type: location
Section: Overview

The snow-covered, towering Mount Kaelorn is considered sacred land. Before the Endless Truce only invokers and their oathsworn were permitted to pass beyond the mountain gate as the final test of the invoker's resolve before entering the Asterreach Ruins.

title: Mount Kaelorn
text:
The Floating Ruins are situated at the peak of Mount Kaelorn. The peak used to be perpetually shrouded in fog, but after the Dreambound Scar became dormant the fog lifted revealing the ruins at the top.

Torren Kaid must hit a target with his skyball to open a new path within the caves.

Reward: Stonehewn Garment Grid, Syndicate Uniform.

Lian and Ayde have returned to Mount Kaelorn, but Garik Rhunari is still feeling resentment about the Sylvan Court. It takes a lecture from Elder Kaelor Rhun to teach him and the rest of the Rhunari to grow by working together peacefully.
page_content:

title: Nerys Quill
text: Nerys Quill is preserved in the archive as a character woven into Elyndran history, ritual memory, and the synthetic world model prepared for retrieval.
page_content:
Title: Nerys Quill
Type: character
Section: Overview

Nerys Quill is preserved in the archive as a character woven into Elyndran history, ritual memory, and the synthetic world model prepared for retrieval.

title: Nerys Quill
text:
Nerys Quill is a slender and athletic teenage girl who has medium-length blonde hair in a high ponytail with two braids hanging down, decorated with orange feathers. Two shorter sections of hair part to her left and frame her face. A pair of bobby pins, one blue and one green, crisscross the left side. She also has the Relicborn trademark green eyes with swirled pupils.

Nerys Quill is also one of the three characters that can fight underwater, along with Caelan Veyr and Torren Kaid.

title: Pyrakar
text: Pyrakar is a humanoid, demonic-looking beast. He has a reddish brown complexion with light red hair, and a bulky appearance. He always appears hunchback.
page_content:
Title: Pyrakar
Type: aeon
Section: Overview

Pyrakar is a humanoid, demonic-looking beast. He has a reddish brown complexion with light red hair, and a bulky appearance. He always appears hunchback.

title: Sanctum of Ordeals
text: Sanctum of Ordeals is preserved in the archive as a artifact woven into Elyndran history, ritual memory, and the synthetic world model prepared for retrieval.
page_content:
Title: Sanctum of Ordeals
Type: artifact
Section: Overview

Sanctum of Ordeals is preserved in the archive as a artifact woven into Elyndran history, ritual memory, and the synthetic world model prepared for retrieval.

title: Sanctum of Ordeals
text:
Aim: To descend towards the Chamber of the Dreambound in order to find Elyra Noctis.

Examine the symbol at the back to make a glyph appear on the right wall, and touch the glyph to open the wall.

Enter the corridor and remove the Glyph Sphere from the recess, walking down the stairs afterwards.

Insert the sphere into the door below to open it, and then retrieve the sphere again once the door has stopped moving.
page_content:
Title: Sanctum of Ordeals
Type: artifact
Section: Details

Aim: To descend towards the Chamber of the Dreambound in order to find Elyra Noctis.

Enter the corridor and remove the Glyph Sphere from the recess, walking down the stairs afterwards.

Insert the sphere into the door below to open it, and then retrieve the sphere again once the door has stopped moving.

title: Selene Marr
text: Selene Marr is preserved in the archive as a character woven into Elyndran history, ritual memory, and the synthetic world model prepared for retrieval.
page_content:
Title: Selene Marr
Type: character
Section: Overview

Selene Marr is preserved in the archive as a character woven into Elyndran history, ritual memory, and the synthetic world model prepared for retrieval.

title: Selene Marr
text:
Selene Marr is stern and scathing (particularly to Torren Kaid), self-possessed, sardonic, moody, cynical, and does not smile often. She is nonetheless kind-hearted, caring, and intelligent and is one of the few Luminous faithful to question the religion on objective terms. Having lost her lover to The Hollow Eclipse, Selene Marr has become reserved and prefers to keep her emotions locked up, choosing to examine the world with realism and logic. She is a big-sister figure to Elyra Noctis, to whom she is fiercely loyal.

Selene Marr is stoic and almost never breaks her stern demeanor. Though usually uncompromising, Selene Marr is close to Torren Kaid since they grew up together and comforts him when he is upset. Selene Marr also has a seductive side to her. For example, if Caelan Veyr expresses an attraction to her, she says that she'll add Caelan Veyr to "her list" and wishes him luck. Selene Marr's personality and figure inspire awe in the group's younger girls, with Elyra Noctis confiding in her and Nerys Quill wanting to be like Selene Marr when she grows up.
page_content:

Section: Details

title: Skyball
text: Skyball is preserved in the archive as a concept woven into Elyndran history, ritual memory, and the synthetic world model prepared for retrieval.
page_content:
Title: Skyball
Type: concept
Section: Overview

Skyball is preserved in the archive as a concept woven into Elyndran history, ritual memory, and the synthetic world model prepared for retrieval.

title: Skyball
text:
Skyball is held in open-air water sphere stadium, designed in a traditional amphitheatre style. Once the sphere is filled, a pair of triangular goals are suspended at opposing points within the sphere's diameter (usually at the north and south horizontal points).

By the time of the Endless Truce the skyball rules are altered slightly:

If a team leads the game by seven goals at any point, they win.

There is only one five-minute round, with no break or half time.

title: Starfang
text: Starfang is a large, black dragon with enormous red wings. He can stand or move in either a bipedal or quadrupedal fashion.
page_content:
Title: Starfang
Type: aeon
Section: Overview

Starfang is a large, black dragon with enormous red wings. He can stand or move in either a bipedal or quadrupedal fashion.

title: Starfang
text: Starfang is obtained after completing the Sanctum of Ordeals in Veylspire Temple.
page_content:
Title: Starfang
Type: aeon
Section: Details

Starfang is obtained after completing the Sanctum of Ordeals in Veylspire Temple.

title: The Hollow Eclipse
text: The Hollow Eclipse is preserved in the archive as a concept woven into Elyndran history, ritual memory, and the synthetic world model prepared for retrieval.
page_content:
Title: The Hollow Eclipse
Type: concept
Section: Overview

The Hollow Eclipse is preserved in the archive as a concept woven into Elyndran history, ritual memory, and the synthetic world model prepared for retrieval.

title: The Hollow Eclipse
text:
The Hollow Eclipse has a whale-like body that it moves with a pair of clawed arms, as well as hind legs resembling pectoral fins for movement in water. It has a long tail and its body is encased in scales it can shed and turn into smaller monsters known as Eclipse shards. It carries part of a city, presumed to be a part of Asterreach, on its body close to the back of the head. The Hollow Eclipse's final form has feathery wing-like protrusions that are purple at the tips.

The Hollow Eclipse can create smaller creatures, Eclipseborn, from its outer layer. Coming in a variety of shapes and levels of power, the Eclipseborn cause trouble for any person that crosses them until The Hollow Eclipse arrives and reabsorbs them.
page_content:

Type: concept
Section: Details

title: The Luminous Order
text: The Luminous Order is preserved in the archive as a faction woven into Elyndran history, ritual memory, and the synthetic world model prepared for retrieval.
page_content:
Title: The Luminous Order
Type: faction
Section: Overview

The Luminous Order is preserved in the archive as a faction woven into Elyndran history, ritual memory, and the synthetic world model prepared for retrieval.

title: The Luminous Order
text:
Temples of The Luminous Order are found throughout Elyndra. At the heart of each temple lies a Chamber of the Dreambound, a room that contains a dreambound —a statue that houses a willingly-given human soul. Through the soul bonding to an invoker mighty creatures called eidolons may be made manifest.

There are five official temples of The Luminous Order, each with their own dreambound:

Veylspire – Home to the dreambound Starfang. Also the central temple of The Luminous Order.

Asterreach – At the Asterreach Dome the invoker can acquire the Crown Eidolon.
page_content:

title: The Relicborn
text: The Relicborn have blond hair and green eyes with spiral-shaped pupils. They dress in distinctive colorful bodysuits and commonly wear goggles and masks. Despite being a marginalized group, the Relicborn have a team in the skyball league and excel at the sport.
page_content:
Title: The Relicborn
Type: faction
Section: Overview

The Relicborn have blond hair and green eyes with spiral-shaped pupils. They dress in distinctive colorful bodysuits and commonly wear goggles and masks. Despite being a marginalized group, the Relicborn have a team in the skyball league and excel at the sport.

title: The Relicborn
text:
Out of all the humanoid races in Elyndra, the Relicborn are the most expressive in their emotions and body language; many of their more notable members also exhibit personalities that may classify as eccentric or juvenile. The Relicborn take an interest in Elyndra's technological past, organizing salvage operations and excavations for ancient technology.

The Relicborn children wear full bodysuits and many The Relicborn wear glasses or goggles to hide their eyes.

The Relicborn have green eyes with spiral-shaped pupils. Regardless of skin color, all The Relicborn have blond hair (assuming they have hair). They are adept at swimming and holding their breath underwater, which serves them well during underwater excavations.

Some words have been adopted from the main Elyndran language, such as voidbeast, magic, eidolon, airship, and The Hollow Eclipse. The name of the people and their language is The Relicborn in their own language as well as in the main Elyndran language.
page_content:

title: The Sylvan Court

page_content:
Title: The Sylvan Court
Type: faction
Section: Overview

The Sylvan Court is preserved in the archive as a faction woven into Elyndran history, ritual memory, and the synthetic world model prepared for retrieval.

title: The Sylvan Court
text: The Sylvan Court have a religious culture, which causes them to come across as arrogant. Their role as oathsworn of the Veilward have led to them looking down upon other races. The Sylvan Court were converted to the Luminous Order religion under their leader, Jyscal The Sylvan Court, who was later appointed a High Preceptor of The Luminous Order. Despite their strict religious culture, they are generous and welcoming to guests.
page_content:

text: On Veyn, the Wardens of Dawn's Lodge serves as an inn for both The Wardens of Dawn and guests to the island. Caelan Veyr is offered to rest there, where he first learns of the Wardens of Dawn's plight to defeat The Hollow Eclipse.

Torren Kaid is preserved in the archive as a character woven into Elyndran history, ritual memory, and the synthetic world model prepared for retrieval.

title: Torren Kaid
text: Torren Kaid has a big heart and frequently attempts to cheer the party up, though he is not always successful. He tends to goof around with the younger members of the group, much to the annoyance of Selene Marr, who berates him for setting a bad example. Though they bicker, Torren Kaid is close to her and affectionately calls her "Lu".
